section_name: Effect on reciprocal recognition of registrations and other reciprocity or proration agreements
text:
(1) The provisions of article 14-B (Bus Taxation Proration and Reciprocity Agreement) and of paragraph (b) of subdivision three of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) shall be applicable only with respect to jurisdictions which are not parties to any agreement this state has entered into pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .

(2) Except as otherwise provided in this section, the provisions of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) governing reciprocal recognition of registrations shall apply to any vehicle not covered by any agreement entered into by this state pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 4
#### Article 14
##### § 405-H — Effect on reciprocal recognition of registrations and other reciprocity or proration agreements

(1) The provisions of article 14-B (Bus Taxation Proration and Reciprocity Agreement) and of paragraph (b) of subdivision three of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) shall be applicable only with respect to jurisdictions which are not parties to any agreement this state has entered into pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .

(2) Except as otherwise provided in this section, the provisions of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) governing reciprocal recognition of registrations shall apply to any vehicle not covered by any agreement entered into by this state pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*

subsections.text: The provisions of article 14-B (Bus Taxation Proration and Reciprocity Agreement) and of paragraph (b) of subdivision three of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) shall be applicable only with respect to jurisdictions which are not parties to any agreement this state has entered into pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .

Except as otherwise provided in this section, the provisions of § 250 (Exemption of non-resident owners and operators) governing reciprocal recognition of registrations shall apply to any vehicle not covered by any agreement entered into by this state pursuant to the authority granted in § 405 (Authority to enter into agreements) .
